items.title: ENGLAND TEAM PREVIEW 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 | ANDY + FPL CHAI! | FIFA World Cup 2026 Fantasy Tips
items.summary:
Kane leads England picks; O’Riley and sub-5% Konsa offer best value routes into defence
- Harry Kane (£10.5m) is England’s premium: pens, 90-minute profile, captaincy option from MD1; reassess before Panama if qualification triggers rest.
- Target England from MD2/MD3: Croatia is the tougher opener, Ghana and Panama are the attacking/clean-sheet swing.
- Buy O’Riley (£4.7m): cheapest likely defender, aerial threat, advanced role upside, top defensive pick.
- Pick Konsa (£4.8m) over Guehi if confirmed starting: 1.1% ownership unlocks sub-5% bonus potential.
- Avoid Rice/Anderson as fantasy picks; set pieces/tackles do not beat attacking slots.
- Treat James, Saka, Rashford, Rogers/Bellingham as minutes risks; use friendlies to confirm starts before buying.

items.title: How to play World Cup Fantasy? - Differences compared to FPL! 🤔 #worldcup2026
items.summary:
World Cup Fantasy rewards active managers: target stat-heavy picks, strong benches and late captain pivots.

- Prioritise midfielders with tackles/key passes and forwards with shots on target.
- Target defenders/keepers for clean-sheet upside; avoid leaky teams with harsher goals-conceded penalties.
- Use under-5% owned players for two-point scouting bonus differentials.
- Build a strong bench; sub out poor starters for players yet to play.
- Captain early high-upside players, then twist to later fixtures if the score disappoints.
- Plan chips around knockouts: Maximum Captain for volatile slates, Qualification Booster for teams likely to advance.

items.title: HOW I FINISHED TOP 1,000 🔥 FPL 2025/26 SEASON REVIEW
items.summary:
Prioritise nailed minutes, Defcon defenders and early Bench Boost planning for 2026-27.

- Haaland remains default captaincy anchor; Bruno Fernandes profiles as premium core pick.
- Build GW1 around nailed starters, penalties, set pieces, strong fixtures and proven underlying numbers.
- Target defensive-contribution defenders; Gabriel, Van Dijk, Andersen, Sessegnon types reduce transfer churn.
- Expect Defcon price rises: Elliot Anderson, Gabriel and Sessegnon cost more and need reassessment at launch.
- Roll transfers when moves are close; bank flexibility for 2-3 move restructures instead of short-term punts.
- Consider Bench Boost in GW1; take differential risks earlier when calls are genuinely close.

items.title: FPL 25/26 SEASON REVIEW! 🔙 26K Rank..! Mavropanos Benched 😂 | Fantasy Premier League Tips 2025/26
items.summary:
Defcon floors beat forward punts; build 2026/27 drafts around centre-backs and defensive mids.
- Prioritise 4-4-2/3-5-2 over 3-4-3; defenders deliver better points per million than forwards.
- Target Defcon assets: Andersen, Chris Richards, James Hill, O’Reilly, Elliot Anderson, James Garner.
- Expect Defcon price hikes; centre-backs outprice full-backs at many clubs.
- Avoid early price-change transfers; wait for injury and team news before selling premiums.
- Lock XI and bench before deadline leaks; late transfers risk Mavropanos-style bench points.
- Chips need stricter execution: bench boost remains the best double-gameweek weapon; wildcard/free hit offer no automatic late rank surge.

items.title: FPL GW38 DEADLINE STREAM ✅ HAALAND CHERKI O'REILLY BENCHED ❌
items.summary:
Bowen becomes GW38 captain call as Haaland, O’Riley and Cherki miss out.

- Sell Haaland for Bowen; captain Bowen against Leeds.
- Salah and Robertson start; buy Salah for a hit only to replace a non-starter.
- Bench O’Riley and Cherki; City attackers pivot to Marmoush/Foden only if chasing.
- Sell Saka; Arsenal rotate before the final. Gabriel is the defender to hold.
- Palmer, Gibbs-White and Szoboszlai are midfield punts; Anthony is the cheap differential.
- Joao Pedro trains and is a hold; Stones is out. Avoid GW38 bench boost planning in future seasons.
